title: Efficient bulk count of distinct authors per category
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.distinct_authors_per_category/0` that returns `{category_id, category_name, distinct_author_count}` for each category. Use a subquery that selects `distinct: [c.id, p.user_id]` to dedupe author-category pairs, then aggregate over the subquery. Or alternatively use `count(p.user_id, :distinct)` directly in a group-by query. Produce the most efficient form. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: `count/2 :distinct` is the right tool; subquery approach is worse. Must know the typed distinct aggregate.
